objective: Tighten harness execution contracts so subagents receive machine-checkable scope, touched paths, and acceptance criteria instead of relying only on loose markdown summaries.
blocking_rule: If the harness cannot prove scope, touched paths, or acceptance evidence mechanically, stop and write BLOCKED instead of guessing.
in_scope: Add a structured JSON run contract template for harness runs.
Teach the harness prompts and runner to treat the JSON run contract as authoritative.
Backfill sprint-002 so the stricter harness flow is demonstrated on a completed run.
out_of_scope: Product backend or frontend feature work outside what already exists for the first slice.
Replacing the harness with a fully autonomous planner-generator-evaluator runtime.
Changing files outside the declared harness paths.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Harness runs have a machine-readable JSON execution contract template.
Harness prompts and runner enforce the JSON run contract as the authoritative scope and evidence contract.
Completed runs can be backfilled to the stricter contract format and still pass local gates.

evaluator_checks: The harness scope is explicit and machine-checkable.
Touched paths are declared for every harness file that changed.
Acceptance mapping uses criterion IDs rather than free-form prose.
The stricter flow still passes on a concrete run.
